resources.string: Which annotation marks a class as a REST controller in Spring?
Which annotation is normally used for business logic?
\@Autowired allows injecting dependencies managed by Spring.
Which annotation is normally used for data access?
Which generic annotation marks a class as a Spring-managed component?
\@Component registers a class as a Spring bean.
Which annotation usually marks the main class of a Spring Boot app?
Which HTTP method is normally used to query data?
GET is used to retrieve resources.
Which HTTP method is normally used to create resources?
POST is usually used to create resources.
Which HTTP method is normally used for partial updates?
PATCH is used for partial updates.
Which HTTP method is normally used to delete resources?
Which annotation allows receiving a variable from the URL?
Which annotation allows receiving query string parameters?
Which annotation allows receiving JSON in the request body?
\@RequestBody converts the request body into an object.
Which annotation allows returning a specific HTTP code?
Which class is commonly used to return a response with HTTP status?
ResponseEntity allows controlling body, headers and status.
Which layer should contain business rules?
Business logic should mainly live in services.
Which layer should receive HTTP requests?
Which layer should communicate with the database?
What does a DTO normally represent?
A DTO transports data between layers or APIs.
Which JPA annotation marks a class as an entity?
\@Entity represents a table or persistent entity.
Which JPA annotation marks the primary key?
Which annotation generates id values automatically?
Which Spring Data interface provides basic CRUD?
JpaRepository provides methods such as save, findById and delete.
Which JpaRepository method saves an entity?
save stores or updates an entity.
Which JpaRepository method searches by id?
findById returns an Optional with the entity.
Which JpaRepository method deletes an entity?
What type does findById usually return?
findById returns Optional because it may not find anything.
Which annotation indicates that a method or class participates in a transaction?
Which file usually contains configuration in Spring Boot?
Which YAML alternative is used for configuration?
Which file does Gradle normally use?
Which annotation defines a bean from a method?
\@Bean registers the result of a method as a bean.
Which injection technique is usually most recommended?
Constructor injection makes testing easier and makes dependencies explicit.
What does IoC mean in Spring?
IoC means the framework manages creation and injection.
What is a bean in Spring?
A bean is an instance managed by Spring.
DI means providing dependencies from outside.
Which annotation allows validating a request body?
\@Valid activates validations on the received object.
Which Bean Validation annotation requires a field to not be null?
\@NotNull requires the value to not be null.
Which annotation validates that a String is not empty or blank?
\@NotBlank validates text that is not empty or blank.
Which annotation catches exceptions globally in controllers?
Which annotation handles a specific exception?
\@ExceptionHandler defines how to respond to exceptions.
Which HTTP status corresponds to resource not found?
404 Not Found means the resource was not found.
Which HTTP status usually indicates successful creation?
201 Created means the resource was created.
Which HTTP status indicates an internal server error?
500 Internal Server Error indicates an internal failure.
\@RestController is used to create REST endpoints.
\@Repository is usually used for data access.
\@Controller and \@RestController are always exactly the same.
\@Component registers a class as a bean.
\@Component allows Spring to manage the class.
\@SpringBootApplication usually goes on the main class.
It is the typical entry annotation in Spring Boot.
GET is usually used to retrieve data.
POST is usually used to create resources.
POST is commonly used for creation.
DELETE is usually used to delete resources.
PATCH is usually used for partial updates.
PATCH modifies part of a resource.
\@RequestBody can convert JSON into an object.
ResponseEntity allows controlling the HTTP status.
It allows returning status, headers and body.
Business logic should be concentrated in the Controller.
The controller should delegate to services.
A Service should be able to use a Repository.
A Repository should handle data access.
A DTO is used to transport data between layers or to the API.
It includes save, findById, findAll and delete.
findById always returns the entity directly, never Optional.
It is a typical configuration file.
application.yml can also be used for configuration.
\@Bean registers the result of a method as a bean.
It helps testing and required dependencies.
Using new inside a controller to create services is ideal in Spring.
The normal approach is to inject dependencies.
The container manages objects and dependencies.
It is a central pattern in Spring.
A bean is an object managed by Spring.
\@Valid can activate validation for a received DTO.
\@NotNull requires the value to not be null.
It is used for global errors.
It defines the response for exceptions.
204 usually means response with no content.
403 usually means forbidden or no permissions.
Spring Boot removes the need to write any Java code.
Spring Boot simplifies configuration, but you still write code.
Complete the code: ____ public class UserController { }
Complete the code: ____(\"/users\") public List<User> findAll() { }
Complete the code: ____ public class UserService { }
Complete the code: ____ public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, Long> { }
Complete the code: ____ public class App { public static void main(String[] args) { } }
Complete the code: ____(\"/users\") public User create(\@RequestBody User user) { }
Complete the code: ____(\"/users/{id}\") public void delete(\@PathVariable Long id) { }
Complete the code: \@GetMapping(\"/users/{id}\") public User find(\@____ Long id) { }
\@PathVariable reads the id from the route.
Complete the code: \@GetMapping(\"/users\") public List<User> find(\@____ String name) { }
Complete the code: \@PostMapping(\"/users\") public User create(\@____ UserDto dto) { }
Complete the code: public ResponseEntity<User> find() { return ResponseEntity.____(user); }
Complete the code: ____ public class User { \@Id private Long id; }
Complete the code: \@Entity public class User { ____ private Long id; }
Complete the code: \@Id ____(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY) private Long id;
Complete the code: public interface UserRepository extends ____(User, Long) { }
JpaRepository receives the entity and id type.
Complete the code: ____ public void transfer() { }
Complete the code: ____ public class AppConfig { \@Bean MyBean bean() { return new MyBean(); } }
Complete the code: \@Configuration public class AppConfig { ____ MyBean bean() { return new MyBean(); } }
Complete the code: private final UserService service; public UserController(____ service) { this.service = service; }
Complete the code: \@PostMapping public User create(\@Valid \@RequestBody ____ dto) { }
A received DTO can be validated.
Complete the code: public class UserDto { ____ private String name; }
Complete the code: public class UserDto { ____ private Integer age; }
Complete the code: ____ public class GlobalExceptionHandler { }
Complete the code: ____(UserNotFoundException.class) public ResponseEntity<?> handle() { }
Complete the code: \@GetMapping(\"/health\") public String health() { return ____; }
It must return a String in quotes.
Complete the code: \@PutMapping(\"/users/{id}\") public User update(\@PathVariable Long id, \@RequestBody ____ dto) { }
Complete the code: \@PatchMapping(\"/users/{id}\") public User patch(\@PathVariable Long id, \@RequestBody ____ dto) { }
PATCH usually uses a partial DTO.
Complete the code: \@Column(nullable = ____) private String name;
nullable = false means it does not accept null.
Complete the code: \@Table(name = ____) public class User { }
The table name goes as a String.
Complete the code: ____(name = \"users\") \@Entity public class User { }
Complete the code: \@Column(name = ____) private String firstName;
The column name goes as a String.
Complete the code: private final UserRepository ____;
repository is the field to inject.
Complete the code: public List<User> findAll() { return repository.____(); }
Complete the code: public User findById(Long id) { return repository.findById(id).____(); }
orElseThrow throws an exception if it does not exist.
Complete the code: public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, Long> { List<User> findBy____(String name); }
Complete the code: \@GetMapping(\"/users/search\") public List<User> search(\@RequestParam ____ name) { }
The name parameter can be String.
Complete the code: public ResponseEntity<User> create() { return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.____).body(user); }
Complete the code: throw new ____(\"User not found\");
Complete the code: \@RestController \@RequestMapping(____) public class UserController { }
RequestMapping receives the route as a String.
The JDBC URL starts with jdbc:.
ddl-auto can use update, validate, create, etc.
Repository handles persistence and data access.
A bean is managed by the container.
The framework controls creation and injection
Inversion of Control delegates creation to the container.
DTO moves data between layers or APIs.
An entity is persisted with JPA.
It extracts values from the URL.
It maps JSON or body to an object.
Find the problem: \@RestController public class UserController { private UserService service; }
The dependency should be injected by constructor or annotation.
Find the problem: \@GetMapping(\"/users\") public void findAll() { }
If the endpoint should list users, it should return a list or response.
Find the problem: \@Service public class User { }
A domain entity should not be a Service
Service is only used in Kotlin
It does not compile because of the name
Entities or models should not be marked as services.
Find the problem: \@RestController public class UserRepository { }
A repository should not be a RestController
Each class should be in its proper layer.
Find the problem: \@GetMapping(\"/users/{id}\") public User find(Long id) { }
The route id must be mapped with \@PathVariable.
Find the problem: \@PostMapping(\"/users\") public User create(UserDto dto) { }
\@RequestBody is used to read JSON from the body.
Find the problem: public class UserService { private UserRepository repo = new UserRepository(); }
You should not manually create the repository
Find the problem: \@Entity public class User { private Long id; }
An entity needs a primary key.
Find the problem: public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User> { }
JpaRepository receives the entity and id type.
Find the problem: \@GetMapping(\"/users\") public User create(\@RequestBody User user) { }
GET should not create resources with body
RequestBody is only used in SQL
POST should be used to create.
Find the problem: \@PostMapping(\"/users/{id}\") public void delete(\@PathVariable Long id) { }
DELETE would be better for deleting
It can throw an exception if it does not exist
Optional.get fails if it is empty.
Find the problem: \@Transactional private void saveData() { }
The transactional proxy may not apply on private methods
Spring applies transactions through proxies; private methods can be problematic.
Find the problem: \@ControllerAdvice public class Handler { public ResponseEntity<?> handle(Exception e) { } }
The method must be annotated to handle exceptions.
Find the problem: public class UserDto { \@NotBlank private Integer age; }
\@NotBlank is used on String; for Integer, \@NotNull can be used.
Find the problem: \@RestController public class UserController { // a lot of business logic here }
The controller has too much logic
Logic should be delegated to services.
Find the problem: \@Entity public class UserDto { }
A DTO should not be an entity
It does not compile because of the name
DTO and entity have different responsibilities.
Find the problem: return null; // when user does not exist
Better return 404 or throw a controlled exception
Spring converts it into an entity
It is better to respond correctly when it does not exist.
Find the problem: \@PostMapping(\"/users\") public ResponseEntity<User> create(...) { return ResponseEntity.ok(user); }
For creation, 201 Created may be better
Find the problem: \@DeleteMapping(\"/users/{id}\") public User delete(...) { return deletedUser; }
For deleting, 204 with no content may be better
DELETE usually responds with 204 or a proper status.
Which HTTP method is usually used to retrieve data?
GET is used to query data.
Which HTTP method is usually used to create resources?
POST is usually used to create resources.
Which HTTP method is usually used to delete resources?
DELETE is usually used to delete resources.
What status does ResponseEntity.ok() normally return?
Which file would you use in Maven for dependencies?
Which repository method gets all records?
Which repository method searches by id?
Which annotation marks a JPA entity?
Which annotation marks a primary key?
Which annotation marks a REST controller?
Order the flow of a typical GET request:
The controller receives, delegates to the service and the service uses repository.
Order the flow to create a user:
First receive and validate, then convert and save.
The call goes down from controller to database.
Order a response when a user does not exist:
First search, then decide the response.
Order the basic Spring Boot startup:
Startup creates the context and beans.
Order the creation of a REST endpoint:
First controller class, then mapping and delegated logic.
The DTO is validated when received.
Order the flow to delete a resource:
Clue: typical Spring Boot configuration file.
Clue: class for HTTP response with status and body.
